page: Amazon Web Services c7i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processors. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The processor configuration includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and matrix operations. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this bare-metal instance, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights the server's exceptional multi-core processing capabilities. In multi-core CPU performance tests, including stress-ng and PassMark CPU evaluations, the instance ranks in the top tier. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read-only small block cached bandwidth of 13985.92 GB/sec and uncached large block read bandwidth of 423.72 GB/sec. It also demonstrates top-tier performance in database operations, static web serving throughput at 117.79 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations processing 191.08 million operations per second. Single-core performance, such as stress-ng single-core CPU, falls within the average tier, indicating that workloads must leverage the massive parallel core count to maximize efficiency.

Given its bare-metal architecture and high compute density, the c7i.metal-48xl offers high qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized, CPU-bound enterprise workloads. The absence of virtualization overhead combined with hardware-level access makes it highly suitable for intensive tasks. Recommended workloads include large-scale software compilation, database operations, high-throughput web serving, cryptography, and CPU-based machine learning inference, as evidenced by its strong performance in processing large-scale language models.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server designed for high-throughput parallel workloads. It features dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processors with 192 vCPUs, 96 physical cores, and a 3.2 GHz clock speed, complemented by 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz. The architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, though it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. In benchmark testing, the instance achieves top-tier results in multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, while single-core performance remains average. With a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and zero virtualization overhead, this bare-metal server is highly efficient for parallelized enterprise applications. It is best suited for high-performance computing, large-scale software compilation, database management, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 192 vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs and 384 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for high-performance parallel computing.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal server delivering massive parallel compute power and DDR5 memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.

384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz
